frameworks.name: dopamine-linked reward sensitivity
frameworks.one_line: eye-blink rate proxies striatal dopamine D2 receptor function

core_question: Does spontaneous eye-blink rate, as a proxy for striatal dopamine tone, predict reward-based decision-making in adolescents?
key_assumptions: spontaneous eye-blink rate indexes striatal dopamine tone
ventral striatum drives adolescent reward hyper-responsiveness
reward learning is captured by RPE-based update rule
manipulations: individual differences in sEBR across 70 adolescents
reward magnitude / risk level in a probabilistic reward task
key_findings: Increasing EBR associated with greater reward maximization in adolescents but not adults
Adolescents showed greater sensitivity to reward value than adults
interpretations: non-invasive sEBR extends animal dopamine-RL evidence to adolescent reward decision-making
limitations_noted: direct dopamine measurement in healthy adolescents not feasible; sEBR is an indirect proxy
